title: Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Batch 1
content_html:
Batch 1: Contract and Dependency Audit

This is the delivery specification for Batch 1 in Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Tasks.

Batch 1: Contract and Dependency Audit

Summary: Lock the source-derived Data Sharing docs metadata contract before implementation touches readers or consumers.

ID status action
1.1 done Audit docs-viewer/services/docs_data_sharing/package.py, docs-viewer/services/docs_export.py, docs-viewer/services/docs_import.py, Data Sharing API wrappers, and focused tests for every current docs index, by-id payload, search, recently-added, or generated artifact read.
1.2 done Produce a field matrix for selectable records, export config field sources, hierarchy expansion, source-text extraction, returned-package review context, preview rendering, warning/error reporting, and activity metadata.
1.3 done Lock the source-derived record shape, including doc_id, scope, title, published, summary, added_date, last_updated, parent_id, parent_title, viewable, ui_status, source_path, viewer URL only where currently exported, source-rendered content/text behavior, and content_text_length.
1.4 done Define helper API names and ownership for the Docs Viewer-owned source metadata read model consumed by Data Sharing service code.
1.5 done Define config-driven scope behavior: records are available for every configured Docs Viewer scope, new scopes are covered automatically, and implementation must not create scope-specific modules or repeated library/analysis blocks.
1.6 done Define missing-source, duplicate-doc-id, unresolved-parent, source-rendering, and stale-data behavior for local Data Sharing calls.
1.7 done Identify all tests and fixtures that currently create, read, or assert public docs index.json, manage/local generated docs index.json, generated by-id payloads, search payloads, or recently-added payloads for Data Sharing metadata.
1.8 done Update this batch with the audit result, final record shape, helper API decision, risk notes, and handoff to Batch 2.

Steer for these tasks

  • This batch is source review and contract locking only.
  • Do not implement compatibility reads or temporary fallbacks.
  • Do not add generated metadata outputs.
  • Preserve current Data Sharing behavior, including published, UI options, package formats, selection behavior, and returned-package review workflows.
  • Treat generated publication artifacts as out of bounds even when they are local, manage-only, private, or checked in.

Deliverables

  • Audit notes in this batch document.
  • Final source-derived record shape.
  • Final helper API/owner decision.
  • List of implementation touch points and tests for Batch 2 through Batch 4.

Audit Result

The current Data Sharing document metadata reads are concentrated in the documents adapter service helpers. The Analytics/Data Sharing API wrappers dispatch through adapter handlers and do not reconstruct docs filesystem paths.

Active generated-artifact dependencies found:

  • docs-viewer/services/docs_data_sharing/package.py
    • selectable_document_records(...) calls docs_generated_reads.read_generated_docs_index(...).
    • document_selectable_record(...) consumes doc_id, title, parent_id, published, viewable, content_text_length, and summary from those rows.
    • the selectable response reports source.source = "generated_docs_index".
  • docs-viewer/services/docs_export.py
    • load_scope_index(...) reads assets/data/docs/scopes/<scope>/index.json.
    • doc_payload_path(...) and load_doc_payload(...) read generated by-id/<doc_id>.json, using content_url when present.
    • build_export(...) uses flat-index rows for selection, descendant expansion, viewability filtering, missing-summary filtering, metadata, hierarchy fields, and direct field mapping.
    • source_value(...) uses generated by-id content_html for headings and source_text.
  • docs-viewer/services/docs_import.py
    • load_current_docs_context(...) reads assets/data/docs/scopes/<scope>/index.json.
    • doc_payload_path(...) checks generated by-id payload existence from content_url or assets/data/docs/scopes/<scope>/by-id/<doc_id>.json.
    • returned-package reports include current index load state, current doc existence, viewability, generated payload existence, parent existence, and parent payload existence.
    • preview rendering uses returned-package rows and current-context flags; it does not need generated payload bodies.
  • Data Sharing API wrappers
    • analytics-app/app/server/analytics_app/analytics_data_sharing_api.py, data-sharing/data_sharing/services/dispatch.py, data-sharing/data_sharing/workflows/prepare.py, data-sharing/data_sharing/workflows/review.py, and data-sharing/data_sharing/workflows/list_returned.py are dispatch/orchestration layers only.
    • data-sharing/data_sharing/adapters/documents/adapter.py delegates selectable, prepare, list returned, review, and apply work to Docs Viewer service helpers.

No active Data Sharing metadata read was found from public index-tree.json, public search payloads, recently-added payloads, or generated payload enumeration as an implicit index. Those paths must still be covered by stale-path guards because they are tempting replacements for the retired public flat index.

Field Matrix

Workflow Current source Fields and behavior to preserve
Selectable document records generated docs index rows through read_generated_docs_index(...) id, doc_id, title, type = document, meta = doc_id, parent_id, published, viewable, selectable, children = [], issues, content_text_length, summary; selectable remains true only when doc_id, published, and viewable are truthy.
Export selection and filters public flat docs index rows explicit selected ids, select_all, descendant expansion, include_non_viewable, missing_summary_only, max_documents, unknown-id errors, skipped reason counts, and selected/exported id reporting.
Export direct field mapping public flat docs index rows doc_id, title, parent_id, summary, current_summary from current summary, last_updated, and viewable.
Export hierarchy mapping public flat docs index rows and computed id maps parent_title, ancestor_ids, ancestor_titles, child_ids, and child_titles, preserving current ordering semantics.
Export rendered-content extraction generated by-id content_html headings from rendered HTML, source_text from rendered HTML before configured transforms, then plain_text_from_rendered_html, omit_code_blocks, whitespace normalization, and truncation behavior.
Export activity metadata export report from docs_export.build_export(...) scope, config_id, target_format, output_written, selected/exported/skipped/failed/truncated counts, error count, and warning count.
Returned-package parse/review context public flat docs index plus generated by-id existence checks current doc existence, current viewability, current parent existence, source metadata from returned rows, duplicate staged ids, malformed row handling, import type detection, warnings for unknown doc ids and missing parents.
Returned-package preview rendering normalized returned rows plus current-context flags summary previews, full-content previews, relationship tree previews, per-record issue lists, preview filenames, dry-run behavior, and safe preview path validation.
Returned-package activity metadata review report from docs_data_sharing.review.review_returned_document_package(...) scope, staged filename, dry-run flag, preview-written flag, detected import type, counts, and preview file paths.

Locked Source Record Contract

The new read model is Docs Viewer-owned and is only for Data Sharing document workflows. The record name for implementation is DataSharingDocsSourceRecord. It is a source-derived projection from scope config plus Docs Viewer source front matter/body rendered through shared Docs Viewer parsing/rendering behavior.

Required record fields:

  • doc_id
  • scope
  • title
  • published
  • summary
  • added_date
  • last_updated
  • parent_id
  • parent_title
  • viewable
  • ui_status
  • source_path
  • viewer_url
  • content_text_length

Required helper behavior:

  • rendered HTML is available through a helper instead of a stored generated payload dependency
  • rendered HTML must use the same Docs Viewer markdown rendering, doc-link rewriting, media token handling, semantic reference handling, and image-title behavior used by docs builds
  • source-text extraction and heading extraction must continue to operate on source-rendered HTML
  • published remains a Data Sharing selectable-record field and must default to true unless source front matter explicitly marks it false
  • source_path is repo-relative and derived from the configured source root
  • viewer_url is included because current export metadata can require viewer URL behavior; consumers should only map it where an active export field requires it
  • no generated_from, source_scope, or generated-output provenance field is added in Batch 2 unless a focused implementation test proves the current diagnostics require it

Do not include speculative fields for reports or future tools. Do not name any file, helper, or response index.json.

Helper API Decision

Add the source metadata implementation under Docs Viewer services. The preferred module name is docs-viewer/services/docs_data_sharing/source_metadata.py because the projection is owned by Docs Viewer but scoped to Data Sharing workflows.

Public helper names for Batch 2:

  • load_data_sharing_docs_source_records(repo_root: Path, scope: str) -> list[DataSharingDocsSourceRecord]
  • load_data_sharing_docs_source_context(repo_root: Path, scope: str) -> DataSharingDocsSourceContext
  • render_data_sharing_doc_html(context: DataSharingDocsSourceContext, doc_id: str) -> str
  • data_sharing_doc_content_text(context: DataSharingDocsSourceContext, doc_id: str) -> str
  • data_sharing_doc_headings(context: DataSharingDocsSourceContext, doc_id: str) -> list[str]

DataSharingDocsSourceContext should hold records, records_by_id, children_by_parent, source docs, scope config, and a render cache. Data Sharing consumers call these helpers and must not reconstruct source roots, generated output roots, by-id paths, search paths, or recently-added paths.

Scope Behavior

Records are produced for every configured Docs Viewer scope returned by docs_scope_config.load_docs_scope_configs(repo_root). The implementation must use each scope's configured source root, nested-source setting, viewer base URL, include_scope_param, and unresolved-parent policy. New scopes are covered automatically by config.

Current configured scopes are studio, library, analysis, and tmp. Batch 2 should test at least one public scope and one local/manage scope, and should include a synthetic config-driven scope fixture so coverage is not hard-coded to library or analysis.

No scope-specific module, repeated library/analysis branch, generated-publication fallback, or adapter registry sources.docs_index/sources.docs_payload_root dependency is allowed.

Error And Stale-Data Behavior

Local Data Sharing calls should fail visibly when canonical source cannot be read or rendered, because generated artifacts are not a valid fallback.

Locked behavior:

  • missing configured source root: raise a service error and return a structured failed report at the Data Sharing workflow boundary
  • duplicate doc_id: raise a service error; do not pick one row
  • unresolved parent_id: follow the scope config; if unresolved parents are allowed, keep the parent_id, leave parent_title empty, and report parent existence as false in review context
  • missing selected doc_id: preserve current export unknown-id errors
  • source rendering failure for a selected/exported/reviewed doc: report the doc-level error and do not read generated by-id payloads as fallback
  • missing generated public flat indexes, manage/local flat indexes, by-id payloads, search payloads, or recently-added payloads: no warning is emitted because those artifacts are no longer Data Sharing metadata inputs
  • stale generated data is irrelevant to Data Sharing metadata and must not influence selectable records, export rows, source-text extraction, or returned-package current-context diagnostics

Returned-package review context should rename generated-payload-specific diagnostics during migration. current_payload_missing and parent_payload_missing should become source/renderability diagnostics, preserving user-facing usefulness without implying generated by-id payload ownership.

Tests And Fixtures To Update

Batch 2 through Batch 4 need focused updates in:

  • docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_export.py
    • currently writes assets/data/docs/scopes/library/index.json and generated by-id payloads in fixtures
    • currently relies on by-id content_html for full-content export and headings/source text
  • docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_import.py
    • currently writes public flat index and by-id payload fixtures
    • asserts current_library.index_path = assets/data/docs/scopes/library/index.json and generated payload counts
  • docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_import_service.py
    • currently writes public flat index and generated by-id payload fixtures for adapter-level workflows
    • adapter registry fixtures still include sources.docs_index and sources.docs_payload_root
  • analytics-app/tests/python/test_analytics_data_sharing_api.py
    • currently writes public docs index fixture for selectable records
    • asserts selectable source is generated_docs_index
    • adapter registry fixture still includes sources.docs_index and sources.docs_payload_root
  • studio/tests/python/test_data_sharing_adapters.py
    • capability fixture still includes sources.docs_index and sources.docs_payload_root
  • analytics-app/tests/smoke/data_sharing_prepare.py
    • mock helpers are named generated_docs_index() and report source = generated_docs_index
  • analytics-app/tests/smoke/local_analytics_app_data_sharing_routes.py
    • selectable mock payload is shape-compatible but should stay source-neutral

Batch 4 should add stale-path guards proving document metadata workflows do not read:

  • assets/data/docs/scopes/<scope>/index.json
  • docs-viewer/generated/docs/<scope>/index.json
  • generated by-id/<doc_id>.json
  • public index-tree.json
  • public or local search payloads
  • recently-added payloads

Risk Notes

  • Rendering source HTML on demand may expose behavior differences from generated by-id payloads if the helper does not reuse the same Docs Viewer renderer path.
  • Source-derived ordering must preserve current export selection and descendant expansion behavior closely enough for package diffs to remain understandable.
  • Review diagnostics currently talk about generated payloads; those messages need deliberate wording updates when they become source/renderability checks.
  • Adapter config fixtures currently describe generated docs paths as active sources even though runtime wrappers do not use them directly.

Handoff To Batch 2

Implement the helper module first and keep consumers unchanged until the source-read contract is tested. Start with source records and context loading, then add render/cache helpers for HTML, headings, plain text length, and export text extraction. Use docs_scope_config.load_docs_scope_configs(repo_root), docs_source_model.load_scope_docs(...), and the Docs Viewer build renderer behavior rather than generated output paths.

Batch 2 should not add generated metadata outputs, scope lifecycle manifest entries, or compatibility fallbacks to generated indexes or by-id payloads.

Implementation and policy guidance

  • Use Development Checklist.
  • Keep source metadata ownership with Docs Viewer parsing/rendering helpers.
  • Keep Data Sharing as the consumer of a service helper, not a filesystem/path reconstruction layer.
  • Make direct dependency updates instead of preserving old generated-artifact reads.

Proposed verification set

  • Source audit only.
  • git diff --check if this batch updates docs.
  • No generated writes.

completed verification

  • 2026-06-05: Source audit completed and recorded in this batch document.
  • 2026-06-05: No generated writes were run for this docs-only batch.
  • 2026-06-05: git diff --check passed.

follow-on tasks

  • Batch 2: implement and test the Docs Viewer-owned source metadata read API.
  • Batch 3: migrate Data Sharing selectable/export/import consumers to the source metadata API.
  • Batch 4: add stale-path guards and remove generated-path assumptions from fixtures.

task close

  • Handoff note added above.
  • Contract is locked for Batch 2.
